content.full_text:
82030+
87207(b)(2)

The term "pro rata share of any income of any

business entity" as used in the last sentence of Section

82030(a) refers to the pro rata share of any net income

of any business entity. The term "fees" as used in Section

87207(b)(2), however, refers to gross fees.

This interpretation was agreed upon by the staff

during discussions relating to Frank Zolin's letter.

sections.conclusion: The term "pro rata share" as used in the last sentence of Section 82030 refers to the proportionate share of net income of any business entity. The term "fees" as used in Section 87207(b)(2) refers to gross receipts.
sections.question_synthetic: The document addresses the interpretation of 'pro rata share' of net income and 'fees' under California Political Reform Act Sections 82030 and 87207(b)(2).
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC clarifies that 'pro rata share' means the proportionate share of net income of a business entity, while 'fees' under Section 87207(b)(2) refers to gross receipts rather than net income.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The document addresses the interpretation of 'pro rata share' of net income and 'fees' under California Political Reform Act Sections 82030 and 87207(b)(2).

CONCLUSION: The term "pro rata share" as used in the last sentence of Section 82030 refers to the proportionate share of net income of any business entity. The term "fees" as used in Section 87207(b)(2) refers to gross receipts.
embedding.first_500_words: 82030+ 87207(b)(2) The term "pro rata share of any income of any business entity" as used in the last sentence of Section 82030(a) refers to the pro rata share of any net income of any business entity. The term "fees" as used in Section 87207(b)(2), however, refers to gross fees. This interpretation was agreed upon by the staff during discussions relating to Frank Zolin's letter.
embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter 79-176 provides regulatory interpretation of key terms in the Political Reform Act, specifically defining 'pro rata share' and 'fees' as used in Sections 82030 and 87207(b)(2). The interpretation was agreed upon by FPPC staff during discussions regarding Frank Zorn's letter.
